steps.title: Identify the repeated change
steps.content:
Each year, the amount increases by $11\%$ of the previous year's amount. That means each new amount is multiplied by
$$1+0.11=1.11$$

steps.title: Classify the function
steps.content: A repeated percent change is exponential, not linear. Since the multiplier $1.11$ is greater than $1$, the values increase over time.

steps.title: Select the description
steps.content: The function is exponential because the same percent increase is applied repeatedly, and it is increasing because the multiplier is greater than $1$. The answer is B.
